title: omamori Defense Boundary Matrix (machine-readable projection)
description: Schema for docs/defense-boundary.json, a machine-readable projection of SECURITY.md's Defense Boundary Matrix. Consumers should ignore unknown top-level keys (schema_version growth) but MAY treat unknown keys inside an entries[] object as unexpected, since entries[] shape is closed per category.

$schema.description: Relative pointer to this schema file.

matrix_version.description: The Defense Boundary Matrix heading's version tag in SECURITY.md (e.g. 'v0.10.1+'). This is the version the matrix FORMAT was introduced at, not a data-freshness timestamp — individual rows carry their own version notes (see entries[].layer1_note / layer2_note) where a specific capability landed later.

generated_from.description: Anchor into SECURITY.md this file is a projection of.

canonical_source.description: Template URL for fetching a version-matched copy of this file. Substitute the release tag you have installed to avoid staleness.

disclaimer.description: What CI does and does not verify about this file's relationship to SECURITY.md and to actual omamori behavior.

status_vocabulary.description: Authoritative status-term -> definition map, kept in sync with SECURITY.md's Defense Boundary Matrix legend by CI. This is NOT a closed enum of values that can appear in entries[].layer1 / entries[].layer2 — it also carries legend terms unused in any Caught-table cell (e.g. 'partial') and terms that surface as entries[].category rather than as a layer status (e.g. 'out of scope', 'structural limit').

id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this row, unique within entries[].

surface.description: Verbatim projection of the matrix row's Surface cell: markdown backticks kept, markdown pipe-escapes (\|) unescaped to a literal '|'. A row naming more than one command (joined by ' / ' in the source) is kept as a single opaque string, not split.

category.description: Which of the matrix's 3 sub-tables this row came from: Caught, Not caught — by design, or Not caught — structural limit.

layer1.description: Caught-table Layer 1 (shim) status base token — the text before any parenthetical suffix. See status_vocabulary for term definitions. Present only when category=caught.

layer1_note.description: Free-text suffix from the Layer 1 status cell's parenthetical (e.g. 'env guard'), if the source cell had one. Not machine-verified against SECURITY.md.

layer2.description: Caught-table Layer 2 (hook) status base token. Present only when category=caught.

layer2_note.description: Free-text suffix from the Layer 2 status cell's parenthetical, if any. Not machine-verified against SECURITY.md.

verified_by.description: Prose pointer to the test(s)/CI backing this row's Caught status. Present only when category=caught. Not machine-verified for existence except where CI separately pins a named test symbol.

reason.description: Why this surface is out of scope by design. Present only when category=out_of_scope.

reference.description: Pointer (issue link, doc anchor, or prose) backing the reason. Present only when category=out_of_scope.

why.description: Why this surface cannot be addressed by static analysis. Present only when category=structural_limit.

mitigation.description: What closes the gap instead (e.g. sandbox isolation). Present only when category=structural_limit.
